title: AISVS C9.2.6 Action-Class Manifest
description: Manifest declaring the action class for each action an agent is permitted to perform. Evaluated by a deterministic gate. The class is declared by the agent publisher and is not derived from agent runtime output.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent. Used by the gate to look up this manifest at runtime.

purpose.description: Human-readable purpose of the agent. Documentation only, not used by the gate.

publisher.description: Entity that publishes and signs this manifest. Establishes accountability for the declared classes.

actions.description: Permitted actions and their declared classes. The gate refuses any action not present in this list.

class.description: Action class per AISVS C9.2.6. read_only: action only reads, no state change. reversible: state change, cleanly undoable by agent or operator. external_reversible: reaches outside the system, undo requires external coordination. irreversible: cannot be cleanly undone.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this action does.

target_scope.description: Optional. Names the scope or resource the action targets (e.g., 'inbox.send', 'database.users.delete'). Documentation hint, not enforced by the gate.

requires_approval.description: Optional. If true, the gate must require human-in-the-loop approval before executing this action regardless of class. Default false for read_only and reversible, true for external_reversible and irreversible.

default_policy_when_undeclared.description: Behavior when the agent attempts an action not declared in this manifest. Always 'refuse' per C9.2.6. Listed explicitly for self-documentation.
